title: The Number Is Information, Not Identity
subtitle: An affirmation for separating the scale number from your sense of self
excerpt: An affirmation from Day 12 of Rebecca's WEIGHT LOSS program. For mornings when the scale number has started running your sense of yourself rather than informing it.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 12 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use on mornings after a difficult weigh-in, or when the number on the scale has become a verdict on your worth rather than a piece of data.

content.text: An affirmation from Day 12 of Rebecca's WEIGHT LOSS program. It targets the moment when the number on the scale stops being data and becomes identity: when a higher-than-expected number means you are bad, failing, or behind.

The scale gives one number at one moment in time. It does not know what you ate, how much water you drank, whether you slept, or where you are in your cycle. The number measures mass. It does not measure worth, effort, character, or direction.
The number is information. It is not who I am.

Say it out loud three times with your feet flat on the floor after stepping off the scale. If it feels untrue, say it anyway. The statement does not require belief to be worth repeating. Return to it on any morning when the number has started narrating the day.
Adapted from Day 12 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
